keyPoints: The Bellman-Ford algorithm can handle negative weight edges but may not work with negative weight cycles.
The Path Vector Routing algorithm is used for finding the shortest path in a network.
In the Bellman-Ford algorithm, we relax edges V-1 times where V is the number of vertices.
The Path Vector Routing algorithm uses a vector to store the shortest distance from the source to each vertex.
The Bellman-Ford algorithm can detect negative weight cycles but cannot handle them.
The Path Vector Routing algorithm is simpler to implement than the Bellman-Ford algorithm.
